rationale: Keep: the cited paper is a close conceptual match to the citing paper’s mechanism space, addressing price-change evaluations via affect vs cognitive inference and showing effects of processing-resource constraints, while the citing paper explicitly tests cognitive depletion/affective processing in price-increase sensitivity. Given this overlap in core constructs (price change response, affective processing, cognitive resources), the citation could be theory/mechanism-supporting rather than a mere incidental mention.
